name: Centeotl
description: Centeotl, also known as Xochipilli (when embodying youthfulness and beauty) and Chicomecoatl (as a female aspect linked to sustenance), is a major deity in Aztec mythology associated with maize (corn). He is the god of maize, fertility, and sustenance, essential to the agricultural foundation of Aztec society. Born from the earth goddess Tlazolteotl after she swallowed a piece of jade, Centeotl's life cycle mirrors the cultivation cycle of maize itself. He is often depicted as a young man adorned with maize cobs and leaves, symbolizing abundance and prosperity. His powers include ensuring successful harvests, providing nourishment to the people, and governing the growth and well-being of maize crops. He plays a vital role in ensuring the survival and prosperity of the Aztec civilization by providing a bountiful food supply. Centeotl's worship involved elaborate rituals, offerings, and festivals celebrating the maize harvest, acknowledging his crucial role in sustaining life. He represents not just the physical sustenance of maize, but also the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth, echoing the planting, growth, harvesting, and replanting of the crop. As Xochipilli, he embodies the youthful energy and beauty associated with the early stages of maize growth, while Chicomecoatl represents the mature, harvested maize, ready to nourish the population. Centeotl's importance extended beyond mere agriculture; he was a symbol of the connection between humanity and the natural world, highlighting the dependence on the earth's bounty and the need for reverence and gratitude.
subtitle: God of Maize, Sustenance, and Fertility

keyMyths.title: Birth of Centeotl
keyMyths.description: Centeotl was born from the earth goddess Tlazolteotl after she ingested a piece of jade. This symbolizes the purity and preciousness of maize.

keyMyths.title: Centeotl's Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, Centeotl sacrifices himself to provide sustenance for humanity, his body giving rise to various food plants. This highlights the importance of sacrifice for survival.

keyMyths.title: Centeotl's Transformation
keyMyths.description: Centeotl's transformation into Xochipilli and Chicomecoatl represents the different stages of maize growth and harvest. He embodies both youth and maturity in the cycle of life.
